# Questions in Livestream 156

Live Stream #156: The Pursuasion Dictionary

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:28</a> | Is Genesis 37 evidence for a clan reading of the patriarch narratives? | He says the already distinct Ishmaelite identity fits a broader clan-development reading better than a very compressed literal sequence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=822" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:42</a> | What name did Egyptians use for the Mitanni kingdom? | He says the Egyptian form is the Naharin or Naharina designation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=1143" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:03</a> | Which Egyptian god was most disliked, why were there no pagan monasteries, and how were stolen tomb goods resold? | He names the Aten as the most disliked, says monasteries are a Christian-era development, and explains that tomb goods were often dismantled or reworked for resale. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=1418" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:38</a> | Where does Ruth fit within the period of the judges? | He places Ruth in the judges period, roughly four generations before David, and points to standard timeline tools. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=1742" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:02</a> | Do the gospel instructions about taking a staff contradict one another? | He says the commands can reflect wording or circumstance differences, while the shared point is dependence on God rather than self-provisioning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=1958" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:38</a> | Was Stephen wrong to say seventy-five people went down to Egypt? | He says this is better treated as a textual-tradition issue tied to Septuagintal counting than as a simple mistake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=2103" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:03</a> | Why are so many Egyptian gods pictured as humans with animal heads? | He says Egyptian gods were understood as personal agents with humanlike traits, while animal features signaled particular divine associations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=2324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:44</a> | Have Muslim Egyptologists tried to explain the Quran's claim that Pharaoh drowned in the sea? | He says Muslim Egyptologists generally avoid the issue; non-Egyptologist apologists sometimes propose Merneptah, but the evidence works against that. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=2517" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:57</a> | What is his favorite song, and can he dance? | He says he has no single favorite song, has eclectic musical tastes, and can dance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=2759" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:59</a> | What is his favorite Bible verse? | He chooses 1 Peter 2:24 because he says it compresses the gospel and atonement into a theologically rich verse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=2944" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:04</a> | What are his thoughts on Bishop Mari Emmanuel's stabbing incident? | He says the attack was a terrible crime, while noting Emmanuel is a controversial preacher who directly challenges false religion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=3382" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:22</a> | Is God omnipresent? | He argues God is not omnipresent in essence, distinguishing God's essence from God's energy and connecting that to sacred-space theology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=3583" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:43</a> | Did ancient Egyptians have gymnasiums? | He says not until the Greek period, though Egyptians did exercise and train outdoors for things like wrestling and martial arts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=3772" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:52</a> | How does Psalm 136 not prove Pharaoh's death in the Bible? | He says the key verb is a traditional rendering, and the text does not require reading Pharaoh himself as drowned. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=4153" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:13</a> | Is yoga demonic? | He distinguishes Western stretching from religious yoga, saying the closer it is to Hindu spiritual practice, the more spiritually dangerous it becomes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=4467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:27</a> | What happened to bodies left on ancient Near Eastern battlefields? | He says bodies were usually looted, gathered into pits, and buried so fields could return to agricultural use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=4811" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:11</a> | What does he think of Maccabees passages about prayers for the dead? | He treats them as a remnant of ancient Israelite cult-of-the-dead practice that Judaism later left behind. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=5445" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:45</a> | What is the supplementary hypothesis regarding Moses' memoirs? | He says the Pentateuch began with base documents, including Mosaic material, that were later edited and supplemented. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=6088" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:28</a> | Does the Greek in Matthew 28 imply one name or three? | He says the singular "name" does not justify modalism; the text names Father, Son, and Holy Spirit without identifying them as one person. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=7104" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:24</a> | What kind of wine did Christ make? | He guesses red wine, roughly 10-12 percent alcohol, and uses the answer to distinguish being inebriated from being a drunkard. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=7455" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:15</a> | What does `pesel` mean in Exodus 20:4? | He says it means a carved divine image or idol, usually made from wood or stone. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=7698" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:18</a> | Is the cup of undiluted wine in Revelation a reference to a Passover cup? | He says it likely refers to the cup of plagues. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=7795" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:55</a> | Are the Sea Peoples mentioned in the Bible? | He says the Bible uses "Philistines" as a catch-all label for the Sea Peoples, not only for the Peleset. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=8302" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:22</a> | Is baptism with fire final judgment, general winnowing, or both? | He says the chaff and unquenchable fire language points primarily to final judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=8482" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:22</a> | Why did the Pharisees expect only the Messiah, Elijah, or the prophet to baptize if mikva'ot were common? | He says they objected to John performing the baptism, not to ritual bathing itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=8777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:17</a> | Where does baptism originate? | He traces it to ritual bathing, initially priestly and then broader, with symbolism shaped by temple-purification practice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=9091" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:31</a> | Does it matter that early church fathers thought Pharaoh died? | He says no, because the fathers are valuable but not individually authoritative or always correct. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=9683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:23</a> | Is 1 Chronicles 22:6-9 saying Jesus is God? | He says the passage is about Solomon and asks for clarification, not seeing Jesus directly in the cited text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=9818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:38</a> | How can Christian laypeople publish scholarly papers? | He says they should find a suitable journal, follow its submission guidelines, and go through peer review like anyone else. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=10155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:15</a> | What are his thoughts on Christianity in North Korea? | He says Christianity is extremely dangerous to practice there because allegiance to Christ threatens the regime's leader cult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=10658" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:38</a> | How much money would need to be raised for an exodus excavation? | He says funding is only part of the issue; such a dig would also need institutional support and permissions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=10857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:57</a> | Could Associates for Biblical Research support an Egyptian dig? | He says ABR's model depends on third-party digs and volunteer fees, which does not fit Egypt's rules. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=10987" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:07</a> | Why has there not been major Christian institutional funding for biblical-maximalist excavation in Egypt? | He says many institutions focus on Israel, do not see Egypt's value, and face Egypt's restrictions on volunteer-funded digs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=12446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:26</a> | How is forgiveness good if unrepentant people can harm again? | He distinguishes forgiving repentant people from releasing vengeance to God, and says forgiveness does not require giving someone a new chance to harm. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=13047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:27</a> | Does the etymology of Samuel's name fit Saul better than Samuel? | He rejects that revision claim, noting Samuel and Saul have different tribal settings and the proposal does not fit the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=13371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:51</a> | What is his assessment of Ay as the pharaoh of the exodus? | He rejects Ay because Ay's location and reign do not fit the required proximity to Goshen during the plagues. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=13888" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:28</a> | Is there historical synchronization between Melchizedek and Abraham's time in Salem? | He connects Salem and Melchizedek to Jebusite deity names and says the Jebusite religious pattern differs from typical Canaanite pantheons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=14915" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:35</a> | Are the Emerald Tablets of Thoth real Egyptian texts? | He says no; he calls them modern New Age fiction with no ancient Egyptian textual basis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=15336" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:15:36</a> | Did all Egyptians ban pork? | He says almost all Egyptians avoided pork, with an exception for the cult of Set, where pigs could have ritual significance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=15992" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:26:32</a> | How do people know what ancient languages sounded like if they have not been spoken for centuries? | He says it is partly guesswork, using living or later cognate languages, Coptic for Egyptian, and comparative phonology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bsrdrbRaZYQ?t=16298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:31:38</a> | Why did Hinduism survive while Egyptian and Mesopotamian polytheism died out? | He says history differed by region: Mesopotamian polytheism gave way to Zoroastrianism and then Islam, while Egypt's was displaced by Christianity and later Islam. |
